page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8369B processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance primarily in the average tier for general compute tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng benchmarks, along with Geekbench and PassMark CPU tests, fall within the middle 50% of recorded results. However, memory performance shows a notable tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 641.24 GB/sec, uncached large-block read bandwidth drops to a weak 36.2 GB/sec. Similarly, LLM inference prompt processing with a medium 7B model is relatively weak at 24.2 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and compute-heavy profile make this instance suitable for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of bundled local storage and moderate memory capacity represent a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. Appropriate workloads include web serving, software compilation, cryptography, and moderate-scale compute tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 24.0 GB of RAM, this x86_64 architecture server provides a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Network performance is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the average tier for most compute, compression, and cryptography tasks, though it exhibits weaker performance in uncached large-block memory reads and medium-model LLM prompt processing. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated compute tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage, making it well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose compute workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for web serving and compilation workloads.

tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute-optimized instance featuring a balanced processor-to-memory ratio for consistent web serving and compilation workloads.

24.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps with no local storage
Average performance in CPU benchmarks, cryptography, and web serving
Weaker performance in uncached large-block memory read operations
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and dedicated compute tasks
